item.text: I’m editing the config layer first. The main change is to make EPAM model selection explicit in resolved config so the command layer can validate and log without duplicating logic.
The type surface is in place. Next I’m moving the selection rules into `ConfigResolver` so `models` and `phase run` both consume the same resolved registry state.
I’m replacing the static model catalog now. `epam models` will read the resolved EPAM registry and surface the configured default instead of hardcoded provider data.

I’m updating `phase run` next. The command will accept `--model`, validate it through the same resolver, log the resolved default path when no override is supplied, and inject the selection metadata into orchestration via env vars.

item.text:
Implemented EPAM model registry and selection across the requested files.

`[src/config/types.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/config/types.ts)` now supports `allowedModels`, `defaultModel`, and resolved model-selection metadata. `[src/config/ConfigResolver.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/config/ConfigResolver.ts)` now resolves EPAM models from the configured allow-list, validates explicit overrides, deterministically falls back to `defaultModel` or the first allowed model, and fixes config caching so flag-based model selection is not stale.

`[src/cli/commands/models.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/cli/commands/models.ts)` now lists the configured EPAM registry instead of a hardcoded catalog and marks the default. `[src/cli/commands/phase.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/cli/commands/phase.ts)` now accepts `--model`, validates it through config resolution, logs default-model resolution when no override is provided, includes model metadata in emitted phase events, and exports the selected model into orchestration context via env vars (`EPAM_MODEL`, `EPAM_DEFAULT_MODEL`, `EPAM_MODEL_SOURCE`, `EPAM_ALLOWED_MODELS`, etc.).

Verification: `npm exec tsc -- --noEmit` passed.

Unit test files were not added because your instructions restricted scope to these four source files, which conflicts with creating or modifying separate test files. If you want, I can do a follow-up scoped to the relevant test files only.
